rag.description: Create a new Google Calendar event on a target calendar (defaults to primary) during an automation run, using either a direct OAuth token or a stored credentials reference. Supports timed events (ISO 8601 dateTime) and all-day events (YYYY-MM-DD date), optional attendees as a comma-separated email list, custom time zones per start/end, and attendee notification control via SendUpdates. Saves the created event Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink as Base64 session parameters under NewGoogleCalendarEvent:* so later workflow steps can reference them without repeating the API call.

qa.question: What does the NewGoogleCalendarEvent plugin do?
qa.answer: It creates a new event on a Google Calendar using the Calendar API v3 events.insert endpoint and stores key response fields in namespaced session parameters for later workflow steps.

qa.question: What are the key features and parameters of NewGoogleCalendarEvent?
qa.answer: Start and End are required. CalendarId defaults to 'primary'. Summary, Description, Location, StartTimeZone, EndTimeZone, Attendees (comma-separated emails), and SendUpdates are optional. Authentication uses Token or Credentials (Credentials takes precedence). Outputs are written to NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink.

qa.question: How does NewGoogleCalendarEvent distinguish between timed events and all-day events?
qa.answer: When the Start and End values contain 'T' they are treated as ISO 8601 dateTime values for timed events. When they do not contain 'T' they are treated as YYYY-MM-DD date values for all-day events.

qa.question: How do I invite attendees when creating a calendar event?
qa.answer: Pass a comma-separated list of email addresses to the Attendees parameter, e.g. --Attendees:alice@example.com,bob@example.com. Use SendUpdates:all to send invitation emails.

qa.question: How does NewGoogleCalendarEvent fit into the integration context of an automation workflow?
qa.answer: It runs as an Action in a sequential workflow step (componentType 'task') using the google icon provider and the ActionRuleModel.

qa.question: What are best practices for using NewGoogleCalendarEvent in automations?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials over a raw Token for long-running runs to allow token refresh. Always provide Start and End in ISO 8601 UTC when creating timed events. Pass CalendarId explicitly when targeting a secondary calendar. Use SendUpdates:none (the default) in test runs to avoid sending spurious invitation emails.

qa.question: What rule or schema details should I follow when configuring NewGoogleCalendarEvent?
qa.answer: Use $type 'Action' with pluginName 'NewGoogleCalendarEvent' and pass at minimum Start and End via argument. Consume outputs from NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink session parameters.

qa.question: What is the manifest key of this plugin?
What manifestVersion is used by this plugin?
What is the pluginType of NewGoogleCalendarEvent?
Which platforms are supported by this plugin?
Which categories is this plugin associated with?
qa.answer: The plugin is listed under the 'GoogleCalendar' category.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin manifest?
What is the summary of NewGoogleCalendarEvent?
qa.answer: It creates a new event on a Google Calendar, supports timed and all-day events, optional attendees and notifications, and stores the created event Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink in namespaced session parameters.

qa.question: How does NewGoogleCalendarEvent handle errors and edge cases?
qa.answer: It accounts for missing or invalid authentication, missing or malformed Start/End values, invalid CalendarId (API returns 404), insufficient OAuth scopes, Google Calendar API quota or non-success responses, empty attendee email entries, and invalid SendUpdates values.

description: ### Purpose
Creates a new event on a Google Calendar.
Schedules meetings, blocks time, or records events from automation workflows.
Supports timed events (ISO 8601 dateTime) and all-day events (YYYY-MM-DD date), optional attendees with invitation notifications, and per-timezone start and end times.
Authenticates via a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference exchanged for a fresh token.
Key event fields from the API response are stored in session parameters for downstream access.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                    | Description                                                                                                              |
| Create timed event         | Creates an event with an ISO 8601 dateTime start and end.                                                                |
| Create all-day event       | Creates an all-day event using YYYY-MM-DD date values for start and end.                                                 |
| Calendar targeting         | Targets any accessible calendar by CalendarId; defaults to the authenticated user's primary calendar.                   |
| Attendee invitations       | Accepts a comma-separated list of attendee emails and controls notification delivery via SendUpdates.                    |
| Time zone support          | Accepts an IANA time zone identifier for start and end independently.                                                    |
| Token authentication       | Accepts a raw OAuth Bearer access token.                                                                                 |
| Credentials authentication | Accepts a credentials id/name and exchanges it for a fresh access token.                                                 |
| Result session storage     | Stores created event fields in namespaced session parameters (NewGoogleCalendarEvent:) as Base64-encoded values.         |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                              | Description                                                                                       |
| Schedule automated follow-ups         | Create a calendar event when a form submission or lead is detected.                               |
| Block focus time automatically        | Add focus or out-of-office blocks to a calendar based on workload thresholds.                     |
| Sync external events to Google        | Mirror events from an external system into a shared Google Calendar.                              |
| Send meeting invitations from bots    | Create meetings with attendees and send invitations directly from automation workflows.            |
| Record SLA deadlines                  | Create an all-day event on the day a deadline is due.                                             |

edge_cases: Start or End is missing or empty
Start or End is not a valid ISO 8601 dateTime or YYYY-MM-DD date
Access token missing required Google Calendar API scope
Invalid or expired OAuth access token — API returns 401
CalendarId defaults to 'primary' when omitted
Summary defaults to a timestamped value when omitted
Description and Location are omitted from the request body when empty
Attendees is empty — no attendee list is sent to the API
SendUpdates defaults to 'none' — no invitation emails are sent
Google Calendar API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure
annotations.expected_result: A timed event is created on the primary calendar and NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink are stored in session parameters.
annotations.notes: Creates a minimal timed event on the primary calendar using a direct OAuth access token.

description: ### NewGoogleCalendarEvent: Create minimal timed event using access token
Create a timed event on the primary calendar by supplying Start and End in ISO 8601 format.
CalendarId is omitted so the plugin targets the authenticated user's primary calendar.
The created event's Id and HtmlLink are available in NewGoogleCalendarEvent:* session parameters.

edge_cases: Credentials id/name not found
Credentials record missing refresh token
Refresh-token exchange fails
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
CalendarId not found — API returns 404
CalendarId refers to a calendar the user cannot write to — API returns 403
CalendarId contains special characters that require URL encoding
Start or End is not a valid ISO 8601 dateTime
StartTimeZone or EndTimeZone is not a valid IANA identifier — API may reject the request
Attendees contains duplicate or invalid email addresses
SendUpdates:all causes invitation emails to be sent to all attendees immediately
Google Calendar API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure
HangoutLink is absent in session when no conference is attached to the event
annotations.expected_result: A timed event with attendees is created on the specified secondary calendar and invitation emails are sent. Session parameters NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink are populated.
annotations.notes: Creates a full event on a secondary calendar using stored credentials, time zones, attendees, and notification delivery.

description: ### NewGoogleCalendarEvent: Create full event with attendees using stored credentials
Create a timed event on a secondary calendar with a description, location, explicit time zones, attendee emails, and invitation delivery.
Stored credentials are resolved at runtime to obtain a fresh OAuth access token.
The created event's Id and HtmlLink are available in NewGoogleCalendarEvent:* session parameters.

rule.argument: {{$ --CalendarId:team@group.calendar.google.com --Summary:Sprint Review --Description:End of sprint demo --Location:Conference Room A --Start:2026-05-15T14:00:00 --End:2026-05-15T15:00:00 --StartTimeZone:America/New_York --EndTimeZone:America/New_York --Attendees:alice@example.com,bob@example.com --SendUpdates:all --Credentials:my-google-creds}}

edge_cases: Start and End use YYYY-MM-DD format — plugin sends date field instead of dateTime
TimeZone parameters are ignored for all-day events (API ignores them for date-only fields)
Summary defaults to a timestamped value when omitted
Attendees is empty — no attendee list is sent
SendUpdates defaults to 'none' — no invitation emails are sent
Access token missing required Google Calendar API scope
Invalid or expired OAuth access token — API returns 401
CalendarId defaults to 'primary' when omitted
Google Calendar API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure
annotations.expected_result: An all-day event spanning a single calendar day is created on the primary calendar and NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink are stored in session parameters.
annotations.notes: Creates an all-day event by supplying date-only values (YYYY-MM-DD) for Start and End.

description: ### NewGoogleCalendarEvent: Create all-day event using access token
Create an all-day event by supplying date-only Start and End values in YYYY-MM-DD format.
When the value does not contain 'T', the plugin sends a date field instead of dateTime, producing an all-day block on the calendar.
CalendarId is omitted so the plugin targets the authenticated user's primary calendar.
Stores the Google Meet or Hangouts video conference link for the created event.
Present only when the event has a conference resource attached by the Calendar API.
outputParameters.name: NewGoogleCalendarEvent:HangoutLink

description: Stores the absolute URL to view the created event in the Google Calendar web UI.
Use it to share or log a direct link to the event.
outputParameters.name: NewGoogleCalendarEvent:HtmlLink

description: Stores the unique identifier of the created event as returned by the Google Calendar API.
Use it to target the event for updates, exports, or deletion.
outputParameters.name: NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Id

description: Stores the status of the created event as returned by the Google Calendar API.
Possible values are 'confirmed', 'tentative', and 'cancelled'.
Use it to branch on the initial event state.
outputParameters.name: NewGoogleCalendarEvent:Status

description: Comma-separated list of email addresses added as attendees to the event.
Each email address is mapped to an attendee entry in the event resource.
Use the SendUpdates parameter to control whether invitation emails are delivered to attendees.
parameters.name: Attendees

description: Unique identifier of the calendar on which the event will be created.
Defaults to 'primary' when not provided, targeting the authenticated user's primary calendar.
Any valid calendar identifier is accepted, including secondary calendar IDs in email-address format.
parameters.name: CalendarId

description: Stored credential record id or name used to obtain a fresh access token.
Avoids hardcoding tokens by exchanging the stored refresh token for a new access token when needed.
Use when access tokens may expire between steps.
Takes precedence over the Token parameter when both are provided.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Event description. HTML content is supported by the Google Calendar API.
Adds context, agenda items, or instructions visible in the event details.
Omitted from the API request body when left empty.
parameters.name: Description

description: End date or date-time of the event.
Use ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2026-05-01T11:00:00Z) for timed events.
Use YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2026-05-02) for all-day events — the value must not contain 'T'.
parameters.name: End

description: IANA time zone identifier applied to the End dateTime (e.g., 'America/New_York').
When omitted, the calendar's default time zone is used.
Has no effect when End is a date-only value (all-day event).
parameters.name: EndTimeZone

description: Geographic location of the event as free-form text.
Displayed in the event details and used by Google Calendar to show map links.
Omitted from the API request body when left empty.
parameters.name: Location

description: Controls whether notification emails are sent to attendees when the event is created.
Accepted values: 'all' (notify all attendees), 'externalOnly' (notify only external attendees), 'none' (send no notifications).
Defaults to 'none' to avoid sending spurious emails during automated runs.
parameters.name: SendUpdates

description: Start date or date-time of the event.
Use ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2026-05-01T10:00:00Z) for timed events.
Use YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., 2026-05-01) for all-day events — the value must not contain 'T'.
parameters.name: Start

description: IANA time zone identifier applied to the Start dateTime (e.g., 'Europe/London').
When omitted, the calendar's default time zone is used.
Has no effect when Start is a date-only value (all-day event).
parameters.name: StartTimeZone

description: The event title.
Controls how the event appears in the calendar UI.
Uses a timestamped default title when no value is provided to avoid creating untitled events.
parameters.name: Summary

description: OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Calendar API.
Used when no stored credential record is available.
Ignored when Credentials is provided.
parameters.name: Token

summary: Creates a new event on a Google Calendar.
Supports timed events (ISO 8601 dateTime) and all-day events (YYYY-MM-DD date), optional attendees with configurable invitation delivery, and per-timezone start and end times.

Authenticates via a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference exchanged for a fresh token, and stores the created event Id, HtmlLink, Status, and HangoutLink in namespaced session parameters.
